q: Real-time mapping of tariff volatility to shop floor MES
diagnostic: A tariff change at the border should trigger a production spec change on the floor within hours, not quarters. A boardroom with Throughput analyst and Capital Steward agents bridges the gap between geopolitics and shop floor scheduling.
answer: A tariff change at the border should trigger a production spec change on the floor within hours, not quarters. A boardroom with Throughput analyst and Capital Steward agents bridges the gap between geopolitics and shop floor scheduling.
description: Synthesis deficit — millions invested in software, but the CEO is still guessing. Systems record the 'what' but nobody synthesizes the 'so what.'
symptom: Five dashboards open, none telling the truth. MES says 90% efficiency but revenue is flat. CRM shows growth but churn is silent.
